name: Avatar Insta Split-Screen Reel
description: Instagram/Reels/Shorts split-screen format: screen-recording b-roll on top (~53%), an AI avatar talking head on the bottom (~47%, auto-cropped from its content band), karaoke caption pills on the seam, a hook badge, a low typing SFX bed over the first ~2s, and a click SFX on every b-roll cut. Built for promo/explainer shorts where a screen recording carries the proof while the avatar narrates.
best_for: 9:16 promo or explainer reels narrated by a HeyGen avatar
turning a product screen recording into beat-locked b-roll under a talking head
comment-gated lead-magnet reels (e.g. 'comment X and I'll send the link')
reusing one avatar render across many topics by swapping the b-roll + beat map
not_for: fullscreen talking-head with no screen recording (use captioned-talking-head)
fictional UGC creator paid ads (use ugc-ai-ad)
news/tutorial masters with source receipts and PiP (use avatar-explainer)

stages.title: Intake + script
actions: Collect the avatar id + voice id (CLI flags or HEYGEN_AVATAR_ID/HEYGEN_VOICE_ID in .env), the screen recording to use as b-roll, and the spoken script.
Shape the script: hook, mechanism beats, then a comment-gated CTA. Keep it tight; avatars speak slower than humans so a 33s human script lands near 40s.

stages.title: Generate avatar clip
actions: Run workflows/avatar-insta-split/gen_avatar.py --script-file script.txt --out avatar.mp4 (9:16, native background, normalized to project fps).
HeyGen renders draw from a separate API credit pool; recover a timed-out poll by video_id instead of regenerating.

stages.title: Transcribe + beat map
actions: Transcribe the avatar audio with Groq Whisper (word timestamps).
Split the narration into beats on sentence/phrase breaks; assign each beat a b-roll in-point and a content-zoom crop of the screen recording (CW:CH:CX:CY).

stages.title: Build split-screen + captions + SFX
actions: It auto-crops the avatar content band into the bottom region, cuts the b-roll on each beat, renders karaoke pills on the seam, overlays the hook badge, mixes the typing bed (first ~2s, very low) + a click on each cut, and loudnorm-exports.

stages.title: QC + export
actions: Sample a frame per beat: confirm no letterbox bars in the avatar region, captions on the seam, badge on the hook, relevant b-roll per beat.
Check loudness (~-16 LUFS), no clipping, no black frames, and that the typing bed is audible-but-low and clicks land on cuts.

routing_keywords: screen recording on top talking head on bottom
comment to get the link reel
script_shape: Hook (one line, why they should care)
Mechanism beats (each beat = one b-roll surface that proves the claim)
Comment-gated CTA (held caption pill at the end)
qc_checks: avatar region has no letterbox bars (content band detected)
caption pills sit on the seam and are time-aligned to the words
hook badge only during the hook window
b-roll changes on every beat boundary and each crop is readable
typing bed is low under the first ~2s; a click lands on each cut
loudness ~-16 LUFS, true peak <= -1.5 dBTP, no clipping, no black frames
compliance_notes: Only use an avatar of a real person with their permission or a licensed/own likeness.
Disclose AI presenters where the platform requires it (e.g. realistic AI content labels).
